frameworks.name: factorised representations
frameworks.one_line: EC encodes separable task dimensions enabling reuse across environments

frameworks.name: graph-structured task representation
frameworks.one_line: behavioural sequences derived from an underlying transition graph

frameworks.name: neural pattern similarity (fMRI RSA)
frameworks.one_line: representational similarity used to test factorised generalisable codes

frameworks.name: generalisation across environments
frameworks.one_line: transfer of learned structure into a novel but partially-overlapping environment

core_question: Does the entorhinal cortex form factorised representations that selectively support generalisation of shared structure across environments?
key_assumptions: task structure is captured by an underlying graph of transitions
some transitions transfer across environments while others do not
factorisation increases neural similarity for shared, generalisable features
manipulations: novel vs familiar environment with partial transition overlap
blocks where transitions were generalisable vs non-generalisable
early vs later exposure to the novel environment
key_findings: accuracy higher in blocks with generalisable shared transitions
generalisation benefit strongest during early exposure to the novel environment
EC patterns differentiated generalisable from non-generalisable aspects
EC starting-location patterns more similar across environments on generalisable trials
interpretations: EC supports generalisation by factorising transferable from non-transferable task dimensions
shared graph structure is represented as increased EC pattern similarity
